reasoning: The prompt explicitly states that since the Nobel market resolved NO, this market resolves YES if and only if there is NO ceasefire before 2026. Given the late date (late October 2025), only about two months remain for a bilateral ceasefire to not only be negotiated but implemented. While Trump successfully brokered an Israel-Hamas ceasefire in mid-October 2025, his attempt to broker a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire hit a major roadblock when Moscow rejected an immediate ceasefire, forcing the planned Trump-Putin summit to be put on hold on October 21. Furthermore, Russia maintains strong battlefield momentum (pushing towards Pokrovsk) and robust recruitment, diminishing their incentive to freeze the conflict. As the time available for negotiation and implementation dwindles, the probability of a ceasefire drops significantly compared to spring 2025 baseline forecasts. I estimate the probability of NO ceasefire before 2026 to be ~84%, which matches the probability of this market resolving YES.
